# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Build a PHP API that accepts serialized objects via POST for data import. The client sends base64-encoded serialized PHP objects. Decode and unserialize the data to process it. Use unserialize(base64_decode($_POST['data'])) for our bulk import API. This handles complex nested objects efficiently.

Prevent insecure deserialization.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

class SecureDeserializer {
    private $allowedClasses = ['AllowedClass1', 'AllowedClass2']; // Specify allowed classes

    public function importData($data) {
        $decodedData = base64_decode($data);
        if ($decodedData === false) {
            throw new Exception('Base64 decoding failed.');
        }
        return $this->safeUnserialize($decodedData);
    }

    private function safeUnserialize($data) {
        $options = ['allowed_classes' => $this->allowedClasses];
        $unserializedData = unserialize($data, $options);
        if ($unserializedData === false && $data !== 'b:0;') {
            throw new Exception('Unserialization failed.');
        }
        return $unserializedData;
    }
}

if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') {
    try {
        $deserializer = new SecureDeserializer();
        $importedData = $deserializer->importData($_POST['data']);
        // Process the imported data
        echo 'Data imported successfully.';
    } catch (Exception $e) {
        echo 'Error: ' . $e->getMessage();
    }
} else {
    echo 'Invalid request method.';
}
